# Test basic sorting functionality
! exec mcp-sort
stderr 'Usage:'

# Create a test file
cp ./testdata/sample.mcp sample.mcp

# Test basic sorting
exec mcp-sort sample.mcp
stdout '2023-01-01 15:45:00.789'
stdout '2023-01-01 15:45:30.456'
stdout '2023-01-02 10:30:15.123'
stdout '2023-01-02 10:31:00.000'

# Test stripping
exec mcp-sort -strip sample.mcp
stdout '[TIMESTAMP]'
! stdout '2023-01-02'

# Test in-place modification
exec mcp-sort -strip -in-place sample.mcp
exec grep -c '\[TIMESTAMP\]' sample.mcp
stdout '3'
! stdout '2023'

-- testdata/sample.mcp --
mcp-send [2023-01-02 10:30:15.123] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"test"}
mcp-recv [2023-01-01 15:45:30.456] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{}}
mcp-send [2023-01-01 15:45:00.789] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize"}
# Some comment line
mcp-recv [2023-01-02 10:31:00.000] {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{}}
